# Hardware Intelligence Report — 2026-07-06

**Generated by:** elect-rix hardware-intel cron feed
**Focus:** Efficiency-optimized compute for local inference (TOPS/W, not raw perf)
**Filters:** Consumer <$500 / SMB <$2000, <50W consumer, can run 7B+ at ≥10 tok/s
**Excluded:** Data center GPUs, cloud-only, vaporware without ship dates

---

## 1. New Hardware Found

### NVIDIA Jetson AGX Thor (generally available Aug 2025)
- **Specs:** 2070 FP4 TFLOPS, 128GB unified memory, 40–130W power envelope
- **Efficiency:** 3.5× efficiency over Jetson Orin (Blackwell architecture)
- **Price:** $3,499 (Seeed Studio dev kit) — above SMB tier, but notable
- **Availability:** Shipping now (Seeed, Yahboom, NVIDIA partners)
- **elect-rix note:** Powerful but exceeds $2000 SMB budget. Relevant as a "prosumer/enterprise edge" reference point. 128GB unified memory can run very large models (70B+) locally. Worth tracking for price drops.
- **Source:** [NVIDIA newsroom](https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/nvidia-blackwell-powered-jetson-thor-now-available-accelerating-the-age-of-general-robotics), [Seeed Studio](https://www.seeedstudio.com/NVIDIA-Jetson-AGX-Thor-Developer-Kit-p-9965.html)

### AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395 "Halo" — Mini PCs & Framework Desktop
- **Specs:** 16-core/32-thread CPU, Radeon 8060S iGPU, NPU, **128GB LPDDR5x-8000 unified memory**, up to 5.1GHz boost
- **Efficiency:** AMD markets up to 7.3× faster than Apple M4 Pro on generative workloads ( Flux, Hunyuan 3D); competitive on LLM inference
- **Price:** Framework Desktop $1,659 (64GB) / $3,149 (128GB); GMKtec EVO-X2 and NIMO mini PCs at ~$1,200–$1,800 (64GB configs)
- **Power:** Mini PC form factor, ~120W peak (above 50W consumer target but reasonable for SMB)
- **Availability:** Framework Desktop **shipping now** ("ships within 5 business days"); Micro Center and mini-PC partners stocking from June 2026
- **elect-rix note:** **Strong SMB-tier candidate.** 64GB config at $1,659 can run 30B models locally with ROCm. Unified memory architecture is the key enabler. ROCm 7.2.1 supports RDNA4/Ryzen AI. The 64GB Framework Desktop at $1,659 is the sweet spot for elect-rix SMB deployments.
- **Source:** [AMD Halo page](https://www.amd.com/en/products/processors/desktops/ryzen/ryzen-ai-halo.html), [Framework Desktop](https://frame.work/desktop), [Igor's Lab](https://www.igorslab.de/en/?p=320905)

### Apple M5 (Oct 2025) + M5 Pro/M5 Max (Mar 2026)
- **Specs:** 10-core GPU with Neural Accelerator per core, 16-core Neural Engine, **4× peak GPU AI compute vs M4**, ~30% higher unified memory bandwidth, 3rd-gen 3nm
- **Efficiency:** Apple Silicon remains the TOPS/W leader for single-stream inference
- **Price:** MacBook Air M5 from $1,099; MacBook Pro M5 Pro from $1,999; M5 Max configurations higher
- **Power:** Laptop-class, well under 50W
- **Availability:** Shipping now
- **elect-rix note:** M5 MacBooks are excellent consumer-tier inference devices. M5 Pro with 64GB unified memory can run 30B models. **M5 Ultra not yet announced** — expected to be the next Mac Studio. For elect-rix, M5 MacBook Air (16GB) is the entry consumer device; M5 Pro MacBook (64GB) is the power-user device.
- **Source:** [Apple M5 newsroom](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2025/10/apple-unleashes-m5-the-next-big-leap-in-ai-performance-for-apple-silicon/), [M5 Pro/Max newsroom](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/03/apple-debuts-m5-pro-and-m5-max-to-supercharge-the-most-demanding-pro-workflows/)

### Raspberry Pi AI HAT+ 2 (Hailo-10H, 40 TOPS INT4)
- **Specs:** Hailo-10H accelerator + 8GB LPDDR4X RAM on PCIe HAT for Raspberry Pi 5
- **Performance:** Llama 3.2 1B at 30–50 tok/s (decode); qwen2:1.5b at ~8 tok/s end-to-end via hailo-ollama
- **Price:** $130 (AI HAT+ 2); Raspberry Pi 5 base ~$80 → **~$210 total**
- **Power:** Well under 50W (Pi 5 + HAT)
- **Availability:** Shipping now
- **elect-rix note:** **Below the 7B/10-tok-s threshold** — only runs 1–3B models at usable speed. Relevant for elect-rix lightweight/special-env tiers where small distilled models suffice. Not a 7B+ deployment target. Software stack still immature (timeout issues reported).
- **Source:** [Hackster.io review](https://www.hackster.io/news/gen-ai-on-your-raspberry-pi-a-hands-on-review-of-the-raspberry-pi-ai-hat-2-3c829a8894dd), [Jeff Geerling](https://www.jeffgeerling.com/blog/2026/raspberry-pi-ai-hat-2/)

### Intel Arc B580 (Battlemage)
- **Specs:** 12GB GDDR6, budget GPU
- **Price:** ~$250 new
- **Performance:** "Surprisingly capable for 7B-13B models" per LocalAI Master; software stack immature
- **Power:** ~190W TBP (desktop GPU — exceeds 50W consumer target when measured at wall, but this is desktop-class)
- **Availability:** Shipping now
- **elect-rix note:** Interesting budget option for 7B inference at $250, but software stack (oneAPI/SYCL) is behind CUDA/ROCm. Only viable for tinkerers. Not a recommended elect-rix deployment target until software matures.
- **Source:** [LocalAI Master GPU guide](https://localaimaster.com/blog/amd-vs-nvidia-vs-intel-ai-gpu)

### NVIDIA DGX Spark (mentioned in AMD Halo comparison)
- **Note:** AMD compares Halo favorably to DGX Spark on GLM 4.7 Flash-30B (+14% for Halo). DGX Spark is NVIDIA's personal AI computer. Pricing/availability unclear from this feed — **needs dedicated search next cycle.**
- **Source:** [AMD Halo page](https://www.amd.com/en/products/processors/desktops/ryzen/ryzen-ai-halo.html)

## 3. Market Signals

- **Edge AI hardware market:** $25–30B in 2026, projected $68–119B by 2031–2033 (CAGR 18–22%). Edge AI *hardware* specifically at $6.65B (2026) → $22.66B (2034), 16.5% CAGR (Fortune Business Insights). Consumer edge growing faster than enterprise.
- **AI hardware (overall):** $151B in 2026 → $691B by 2033 (Grand View). The edge/consumer slice is small but growing fastest.
- **Computex 2026 theme:** "AI Everywhere" — proliferation of mini PCs with NPUs. LTT Labs notes the non-Pro NUC ships with Intel Core Series 3 (Wildcat Lake), Pro NUC 16 up to Core Ultra X9 388H. Mini-PC AI capability is real but buyers shouldn't pay for NPU marketing unless software supports it.
- **Software maturity gap persists:** Snapdragon X Elite NPU underperforms CPU for LLM inference (software, not silicon). ROCm on RDNA4 "disappointing" vs expectations but 2–10× faster than 6.4.2. Intel Arc needs oneAPI work. **CUDA (NVIDIA) and Metal (Apple) remain the only mature stacks for local LLM inference.**
- **Consumer adoption:** Reddit r/LocalLLaMA active discussion on Mac Studio/M4 for local inference; distilled models (DeepSeek-R1 1.5B, Phi-3 Mini) making SBC inference viable in 2026 where it was a "parlor trick" before.
- **Groq LPU → acquired by NVIDIA.** Groq 3 LPU announced at GTC 2026, targeting 1500 tok/s for agentic AI, paired with Vera Rubin NVL72. **This is data-center/cloud only — no consumer device. Filtered out per criteria** but signals the inference-performance frontier is moving fast, which will trickle down.

---

## 4. Deployment Implications for elect-rix

### Tier 1 — Consumer Edge (under $500, <50W, 7B at ≥10 tok/s)
| Device | Verdict |
|---|---|
| **Jetson Orin Nano Super ($249)** | ✅ **Primary recommendation.** 67 TOPS, 7–15W, confirmed ~10–15 tok/s on 7B Q4–Q6. CUDA stack mature. Can run elect-rix special environments today. |
| Raspberry Pi 5 + AI HAT+ 2 ($210) | ⚠️ Only 1–3B models at usable speed. Use for lightweight/distilled special envs only. |
| Orange Pi 5 Pro ($120) | ❌ NPU too weak (6 TOPS), software immature. CPU inference too slow for 7B. |

### Tier 2 — SMB/Prosumer (under $2000, reasonable power, 13B+ models)
| Device | Verdict |
|---|---|
| **Framework Desktop 64GB ($1,659)** | ✅ **Primary SMB recommendation.** 128GB-unified-memory architecture at SMB price. ROCm 7.2.1 supported. Can run 30B models. |
| Apple M5 Pro MacBook 64GB ($1,999+) | ✅ Excellent efficiency, mature Metal stack. Premium price but best TOPS/W. |
| AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395 mini PC (64GB, ~$1,200–$1,800) | ✅ Alternative to Framework; check specific OEM thermals. |
| Intel Arc B580 ($250) + mini PC | ⚠️ Budget option but software immaturity is a deployment risk. |

### Tier 3 — Enterprise Edge (above $2000, reference only)
- Jetson AGX Thor ($3,499): 128GB, Blackwell. Reference for what's possible at the edge.
- Mac Studio M3 Ultra (256GB, ~$4,000+): Best for very large local models. M5 Ultra anticipated.

### Key Deployment Notes
1. **CUDA/Metal maturity matters more than raw TOPS.** Recommend Jetson (CUDA) for Tier 1 and Apple Silicon (Metal) or AMD (ROCm, now usable) for Tier 2.
2. **Unified memory is the differentiator.** Devices with unified memory (Apple, Ryzen AI Max, Jetson) can load much larger models than discrete-GPU systems at the same price.
3. **NPU marketing ≠ LLM capability.** Snapdragon X Elite NPU and Hailo NPUs are optimized for vision/captioning, not autoregressive LLM inference. CPU is often faster for LLMs. Don't spec elect-rix deployments around NPU TOPS alone.
4. **7B at 10 tok/s is achievable today at $249** (Jetson Orin Nano Super). This is the floor for elect-rix consumer special environments.
